methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $19M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Foundry Robotics, a Seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Adarsh Kulkarni, has raised $19M to date. The broader robotics and manufacturing sector includes 1 public comparable (ticker 277810.KQ) and 8 private peers, among them 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics. However, the peer set lacks sufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables meeting minimum density thresholds, limiting the applicability of median-based peer valuation.

The Stage-Progression method is the applicable valuation framework here. Using total capital raised ($19M) as a proxy for base value and applying a typical 2.5x step-up multiplier from Seed to the subsequent financing round yields an implied valuation of $48M. This methodology reflects observed step-up dynamics within frontier-tech funding patterns rather than company-specific operational metrics.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $40M to $55M, with a median of $48M. This range reflects only a single methodological approach, introducing material reliance risk. Key uncertainties include the absence of narrow peer comparables to validate stage-transition assumptions, undisclosed revenue or unit economics that would support revenue-multiple or DCF-based approaches, and the inherent volatility of early-stage step-up multiples across market conditions. Investors should treat this range as preliminary pending access to operational traction and refined peer benchmarking.
